scope: S4096 layer-2 actual LightningIndexer route, source-boundary sparse-MLA backward dKV/score ownership probes

baseline_gates.read: A promotable cooperative kernel has to beat or clearly approach the Triton source-boundary full-backward gate, not just a late materialized dKV-only slice.

measurements.name: row_leader_ck_sharedkv_score_page_seq16_score_only
measurements.decision: reject as too slow versus Triton full boundary

measurements.name: row_leader_ck_sharedkv_score_page_seq1_score_only
measurements.decision: reject; smaller token serialization helps only modestly

measurements.name: query_headblock_ck_dkv_source_boundary
measurements.decision: reject; source-boundary recompute without grouped write reduction is worse than row-leader and Triton

mori_shmem_read.applicability: MORI shmem/IR exposes device-side P2P/RDMA and dispatch/combine communication primitives.
mori_shmem_read.not_applicable_for: single-GPU sibling-CTA score-tile sharing or replacing LDS/register lifetime inside a sparse-attention backward kernel
mori_shmem_read.use_for_parallel_track: EP dispatch/combine, AsyncLL split send/recv, and dX return overlap/skew reduction in the MoE path

decision.reason: Both row-leader score-page and naive query/head-block CK source-boundary schedules lose badly. The query/head-block diagnostic preserved source-boundary inputs but achieved no route coalescing, so it paid expensive recompute and still issued the full indirect write pattern.
decision.next_kernel_shape: Keep a Triton-like query-major producer, but make the epilogue group-aware inside the same owner: token/head/selected-window CTA, compact route metadata, local segmented reduction by compressed group before global dKV writes, while avoiding D16 replay of QK/PV.
decision.non_goal: Do not pursue MORI shmem as a score-page sharing mechanism for sparse MLA; it is a communication primitive, not intra-kernel shared memory.
